title: Buzzing About World Mosquito Day
story: Every August 20, people around the world celebrate World Mosquito Day by learning about these tiny but important insects. Scientists share cool facts about how mosquitoes help pollinate plants and feed fish and birds. Museums host bug exhibits, and kids make mosquito crafts or play educational games. Some communities even organize clean-up events to remove standing water where mosquitoes breed, making neighborhoods healthier for everyone!
activity_question: What do some communities do on World Mosquito Day to make neighborhoods healthier?
activity_choices: Organize clean-up events to remove standing water
Release pet frogs to eat mosquitoes
activity_answer: Organize clean-up events to remove standing water
bonus_fact: Only female mosquitoes bite humans - males prefer flower nectar!

title: Tune In to National Radio Day!
story: Every August 20, radio lovers celebrate National Radio Day by cranking up their favorite stations! People share memories of car sing-alongs, morning talk shows, and late-night music discoveries on social media. Radio DJs often host special contests and play listener requests all day long. Some families even have old-fashioned radio parties, gathering around vintage radios to listen together like people did decades ago. It's amazing how this simple invention still connects millions of people through music, news, and stories every single day!
activity_question: How do radio DJs often celebrate National Radio Day?
activity_choices: By hosting special contests and playing listener requests
By only playing classical music all day
By giving away free radios to everyone
activity_answer: By hosting special contests and playing listener requests
follow_up_question: What's your favorite thing to listen to on the radio?

title: Pie-fect Chocolate Pecan Paradise Day
story: Every August 20, dessert lovers across America celebrate National Chocolate Pecan Pie Day by indulging in this ultimate sweet treat! Bakeries showcase their fanciest chocolate pecan pies, while home bakers dust off grandma's recipes to create gooey, nutty masterpieces. The holiday combines two beloved flavors - rich chocolate and crunchy pecans - in one incredible dessert. Restaurants often feature special chocolate pecan pie deals, and social media fills with drool-worthy photos of golden crusts filled with chocolatey pecan goodness. Some families make it a tradition to bake together, creating memories as sweet as the pie itself!
activity_question: What two main flavors are combined in chocolate pecan pie?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite pie flavor combination?

title: Virtual Vacation Day: Travel Without Leaving Home!
story: Pack your imagination instead of your suitcase because August 20 is National Virtual Vacation Day! People celebrate by taking pretend trips without leaving their homes. Families use computers to virtually tour famous museums, walk through national parks, or explore ancient castles online. Some people create themed dinners matching their dream destinations, like making tacos for a "trip" to Mexico or pasta for "visiting" Italy. Kids build blanket forts that become tropical islands or space stations. Social media fills with photos of people wearing vacation clothes while sitting in their living rooms, pretending they're at the beach or mountains.
activity_question: What do people do to celebrate National Virtual Vacation Day?
activity_choices: Take real trips to other countries
Use computers to virtually tour places and create themed experiences at home
Only watch travel shows on TV
Sleep all day to dream about vacations
activity_answer: Use computers to virtually tour places and create themed experiences at home
follow_up_question: If you could take a virtual vacation anywhere, where would you go and what would you want to see?
